name: GT-orderunit-def
source: HOS, refs/hos/joa-m.md:368-372 (1.2.1, order unit space and order norm), VERBATIM: "An element  $e \in A^+$  is called an order unit for A if for all  $a \in A$  there is  $\lambda > 0$  such that  $-\lambda e \le a \le \lambda e$ . ... In this case A has a norm given by  $||a|| = \inf\{\lambda > 0: -\lambda e \le a \le \lambda e\}$ ." Hence for self-adjoint x: ||x||<=c iff -c*1 <= x <= c*1; and ||x-y||<=eps gives -eps*1 <= x-y <= eps*1, i.e. x >= y - eps*1.
